name: Couinaud liver segments
review_note: Standard anatomy; not yet clinician-verified. Watch anterior/posterior handling of Segment I.

figure_scopes.overview: All segments I-VIII with hepatic vein planes in one plate, optional posterior inset for Segment I
figure_scopes.closeup: A specific sector or segment with its bounding hepatic vein plane

laterality: Standard anterior view: viewer-left contains the right hemiliver segments; Segment I is posterior near the IVC
must_have: Segments I, II, III, IVa, IVb, V, VI, VII, and VIII.
Middle hepatic vein, right hepatic vein, and left hepatic vein.
spatial_rules: Use standard Couinaud labels: Segment I, II, III, IVa, IVb, V, VI, VII, VIII.
Right anterior sector is VIII over V; right posterior sector is VII over VI.
Left lateral sector is II over III; left medial sector is IVa over IVb.
In the standard anterior view, viewer-left contains the right hemiliver segments.
Right hepatic vein separates right anterior/posterior sectors; middle hepatic vein separates right and left hemiliver along the Cantlie line; left hepatic vein separates left medial/lateral sectors.
Segment I is posterior near the IVC and not on the anterior free surface.

forbidden_errors.error: Posterior Segment I placed on the anterior liver surface.
Segment ownership swapped relative to the hepatic vein planes.

safety_concept: Hepatic vein planes (not portal pedicles) define segment boundaries; respect the Cantlie line and Segment I posterior position.
verify: Couinaud segment ownership matches the hepatic vein planes.
Right anterior sector is VIII over V; right posterior sector is VII over VI; left lateral is II over III; left medial is IVa over IVb.
Segment I is posterior near the IVC, not on the anterior free surface.
